concerns.text: T023 acknowledges 'no data source is defined' for the FR-008 Global Exposure fallback. The task only sets a flag and logs a warning, effectively dropping the FR-008 requirement to 'calculate a Global Exposure metric'. This is a silent relaxation of a functional requirement.

EC-001 mandates the fallback check (>50% missing birth years) occur BEFORE the Minimum Listen Threshold filter. T023 (check) depends on T013a (filter_cohort) and T015 (frequency filter) in the orchestration logic described in T028. This reverses the required order, violating the edge case constraint and risking false fallback triggers.

SC-004 requires a warning if match rate < 80%. T036 implements logic to treat the config value '[deferred]' as a string and log a 'check was deferred' warning, bypassing the numeric comparison against 0.8. This silently weakens the success criterion validation when the config is not explicitly updated.

FR-006 requires re-running the 'entire matching and aggregation process' including re-calculation of exposure scores if the track set changes. T044 explicitly loads pre-computed `ingested_cohort.parquet` (T018) and only re-runs matching/aggregation, failing to re-calculate `residualized_exposure_score` which depends on the track list. This violates the sensitivity analysis requirement.

T057 (Effect Size) and T058 (Assumption Validation) are listed as active tasks but are not required by any Functional Requirement in spec.md (FR-005 only specifies the model formula). Implementing these constitutes scope creep and violates the constraint that tasks must not introduce requirements not present in the spec.

FR-007 requires a block-permutation test shuffling scores among tracks while preserving User-Track grouping. T045a describes aggregating scores to track level and shuffling the vector, but the description is ambiguous regarding the re-join step required to preserve the exact number of pairs per track (the 'block' structure). This risks violating the permutation methodology constraint.
